commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex workspace prompt-surface and personality config, persists prompt metadata for binding rotation, updates docs/tests/live proof, and includes a small OpenRouter object-spread lint cleanup.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding at source level: the PR leaves the native project-doc budget undefined when no override exists, while upstream Codex defaults it to 32 KiB. I did not run a live default-budget repro.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can make the fingerprint mirror honor Codex's default 32 KiB project-doc budget and add a regression test.

target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable
target.body:
## Summary This rebuild turns the Codex workspace prompt routing from a forced token-efficiency change into an explicit, user-selectable prompt-surface policy. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.workspacePromptSurface`: - `per_turn_context` (default): quality-first legacy behavior. `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain fresh current-turn context. - `thread_developer`: opt-in token-efficient/high-priority mode. Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. OpenAI's Codex prompt write-up describes native project-doc loading with `AGENTS.override.md`, `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback filenames, and the `project_doc_max_bytes` cap: https://openai.com/index/unrolling-the-codex-agent-loop/ ## Authority and lifetime | Surface | Owner | Files / content | Lifetime | This PR | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Codex base/model instructions | Codex | Codex system/model stack | Native thread | OpenClaw does not override | | Codex personality slot | Codex protocol | `friendly`, `pragmatic`, `none` | Native thread/turn param | Configurable via `personalityMode`; defaults to `none` only when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered | | OpenClaw developer bridge | OpenClaw | Runtime/delivery/tool/delegation bridge | Native thread | Kept narrow and host-owned | | OpenClaw per-turn context | OpenClaw | Soul/profile/memory/bootstrap/skills/channel context | Every turn | Default quality-first surface | | Codex thread developer instructions | OpenClaw -> Codex | Stable soul/profile files only | Native thread | Opt-in `thread_developer`, with fingerprint rotation | | Native Codex project docs | Codex | `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, fallbacks | Native project-doc load | Preserved; no `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression | | Heartbeat collaboration pointer | OpenClaw | Pointer to `HEARTBEAT.md` | Heartbeat turn | Still separate from soul/profile | ## Scenario coverage | Scenario | Expected behavior | | --- | --- | | Default Codex app-server run with non-empty `SOUL.md` | Stable profile files are current-turn context; Codex native personality is disabled; bridge points personality at `SOUL.md`. | | Default Codex app-server run with empty `SOUL.md` | Empty placeholder content does not suppress Codex's native personality overlay. | | User edits `SOUL.md` mid-session in default mode | Next turn sees the edit because the file remains per-turn context. | | User opts into `thread_developer` | Stable profile files move to developer instructions; `MEMORY.md` and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain turn context. | | User edits `SOUL.md` in `thread_developer` mode | OpenClaw fingerprints the tracked developer files and starts a fresh native Codex thread. | | User edits `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` | OpenClaw fingerprints loaded native project-doc content and rotates the native Codex binding. | | Lightweight cron Codex run | OpenClaw still skips extra bootstrap prompt text for exact command preservation, does not disable native Codex project docs, and does not send `personality: "none"` unless the user explicitly configured `personalityMode: "none"`. | | Existing stale binding from the previous PR | Missing prompt-surface metadata forces a new native Codex thread, so old bindings do not preserve stale developer/user context. | | Manual `/codex resume ` binding | The minimal manual binding resumes once, then persists the current prompt-surface/personality metadata after successful resume. | ## Implementation notes - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts` adds the new config enums and resolvers. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` selects the workspace prompt surface, computes the workspace fingerprint, chooses the positive SOUL bridge behavior, and keeps native project docs out of OpenClaw turn text. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` carries `personality`, `workspacePromptSurface`, and `workspacePromptFingerprint` through start/resume params and binding invalidation. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` persists the prompt-surface metadata. - Docs now describe the architecture in `docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`, and `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md`. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: Codex workspace prompt routing is selectable, defaults to fresh per-turn profile context, rotates stale native Codex bindings when native project docs or thread-developer profile files change, and only disables Codex's native personality when non-empty SOUL context is actually delivered or the user explicitly forces `personalityMode: "none"`. - Current narrowed head tested: `22146a4c0e` on branch `codex/workspace-context-routing-v2`, rebased on `upstream/main` `ac69776330`. - Scope note: this head removes the prior fs-safe default flip, archive fallback, Windows ACL policy change, and dependency expectation cleanup from the PR. The only non-Codex file left is `extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts`, a behavior-preserving object-spread lint cleanup required because the repo's fail-safe changed gate lints the extension surface on this branch. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`, using a temporary OpenClaw gateway and Codex app-server model route. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash OPENCLAW_LIVE=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_MODEL=codex/gpt-5.4-mini \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_DEBUG=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \ node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts -t "project_doc_max_bytes" ``` - Evidence after fix: ```text [gateway-codex-live] project-doc-budget-binding-proof { "hiddenEditKeptThread": true, "visibleEditRotatedThread": true, "first": { "threadId": "019e45bc-e4db-7ed3-96a8-aa60520f2d98", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "b069dc8610d184a93eeddac87043c5f93b5b32a9862b503a93b4145773b3e0c4", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "hidden": { "threadId": "019e45bc-e4db-7ed3-96a8-aa60520f2d98", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "b069dc8610d184a93eeddac87043c5f93b5b32a9862b503a93b4145773b3e0c4", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "visible": { "threadId": "019e45bd-16d9-7201-9f49-97f91fc03154", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "9bf7dafa05fdb8a041fadb9190e9505f8b0b8b6402cdede2ce424d727a35ec96", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" } } Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 2 skipped (3) Duration 115.55s ``` - Observed result after fix: With `project_doc_max_bytes = 8`, editing only bytes outside Codex's native project-doc budget kept the same OpenClaw Codex binding/thread/fingerprint. Editing bytes inside the native budget rotated both the native thread and workspace prompt fingerprint. This proves OpenClaw's binding invalidation now matches Codex's native project-doc visibility on the current narrowed head. - What was not tested: Broad cross-platform and full-matrix coverage are left to GitHub Actions; the live proof used local macOS Codex app-server auth/model access. ## Validation Current-head focused validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts \ src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts ``` Result: 5 test files passed, 300 tests passed. Changed-gate validation: ```bash node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs -- extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts pnpm check:changed ``` Result: passed. `pnpm check:changed` completed typecheck, lint, dependency/package guards, duplicate coverage, runtime sidecar loader guard, conflict-marker/changelog checks, and runtime import-cycle checks. ## Maintainer review notes This PR intentionally keeps the Codex-native lane narrow: - It does not change harness selection policy, model routing, auth routing, or Codex app-server transport behavior. - It preserves native Codex base/model instructions and native project-doc loading instead of replacing them with OpenClaw prompt text. - It keeps quality-first per-turn profile context as the default and makes token-efficient thread developer instructions opt-in. - It rotates native Codex bindings when the selected prompt surface, personality policy, visible native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. - It leaves volatile `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skill context in the per-turn lane even when stable soul/profile files are opted into thread developer instructions.
target.author: 100yenadmin
